evals.name: pr-review
evals.prompt:
帮我看一下这段代码有什么问题：

```python
class UserService:
    def update_profile(self, user_id, name, email, avatar_url):
        user = self.db.query(f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = {user_id}")
        user['name'] = name
        user['email'] = email
        user['avatar_url'] = avatar_url
        self.db.execute(f"UPDATE users SET name='{name}', email='{email}', avatar_url='{avatar_url}' WHERE id={user_id}")
        
        # send notification
        if user['email'] != email:
            self.smtp.send(email, "Email changed", f"Hi {name}, your email was updated.")
        
        # update loyalty points
        points = user['login_count'] * 10 + 500
        self.db.execute(f"UPDATE loyalty SET points={points} WHERE user_id={user_id}")
        
        # invalidate cache
        self.redis.delete(f"user:{user_id}")
        self.redis.delete(f"user:email:{user['email']}")
        self.redis.delete(f"loyalty:{user_id}")
        
        return {"status": "ok"}
```
evals.expected_output: PR Review report with Health Score, findings including Change Propagation (Divergent Change), Cognitive Overload. Each finding must have Symptom/Source/Consequence/Remedy format. Must reference specific book titles.

evals.name: architecture-audit
evals.prompt:
请审计一下我们项目的架构，目录结构如下：

```
src/
├── controllers/
│   ├── UserController.ts      # imports from services/ AND from models/ AND from lib/postgres.ts
│   └── OrderController.ts     # imports from services/ AND directly from lib/stripe.ts
├── services/
│   ├── UserService.ts         # imports from models/ AND from lib/redis.ts
│   ├── OrderService.ts        # imports from models/ AND from services/UserService.ts AND from lib/postgres.ts
│   └── NotificationService.ts # imports from services/UserService.ts AND from services/OrderService.ts
├── models/
│   ├── User.ts                # imports from services/NotificationService.ts  (to send welcome email on create)
│   └── Order.ts               # imports from models/User.ts
└── lib/
    ├── postgres.ts
    ├── redis.ts
    └── stripe.ts
```
evals.expected_output: Architecture Audit report with module dependency map, findings including Dependency Disorder (circular dependency: models→services→models, DIP violations). Each finding must have Symptom/Source/Consequence/Remedy format.

evals.name: tech-debt
evals.prompt: 我们的订单系统越来越难维护，每次加新功能都得改 OrderService，不管是加支付方式、还是改通知逻辑、还是调库存计算，全在那一个文件里。而且这个文件只有我一个人敢改，新来的工程师都说看不懂。帮我评估一下有哪些技术债务。
evals.expected_output: Tech Debt Assessment with Pain × Spread scoring, findings including Change Propagation and Domain Model Distortion. Should include Debt Summary Table. Each finding must have Symptom/Source/Consequence/Remedy format.

evals.name: test-quality-review
evals.prompt:
帮我看一下这些测试有什么问题：

```python
# test_order_service.py

class TestOrderService:
    def setup_method(self):
        self.mock_db = Mock()
        self.mock_payment = Mock()
        self.mock_inventory = Mock()
        self.mock_notification = Mock()
        self.mock_logger = Mock()
        self.mock_cache = Mock()
        self.service = OrderService(
            self.mock_db, self.mock_payment,
            self.mock_inventory, self.mock_notification,
            self.mock_logger, self.mock_cache
        )

    def test_order(self):
        self.mock_db.get_user.return_value = {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice'}
        self.mock_inventory.check.return_value = True
        self.mock_payment.charge.return_value = {'status': 'ok', 'tx_id': 'abc'}
        result = self.service.create_order(1, 'item_99', 2)
        assert result['status'] == 'created'
        assert self.mock_payment.charge.called
        assert self.mock_notification.send.called
        assert self.mock_db.save_order.called
        assert self.mock_cache.invalidate.called

    def test_order2(self):
        self.mock_db.get_user.return_value = {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice'}
        self.mock_inventory.check.return_value = True
        self.mock_payment.charge.return_value = {'status': 'ok', 'tx_id': 'xyz'}
        result = self.service.create_order(1, 'item_99', 2)
        assert result['status'] == 'created'
        assert self.mock_payment.charge.called
        assert self.mock_notification.send.called

    def test_payment_failure(self):
        self.mock_db.get_user.return_value = {'id': 1, 'name': 'Alice'}
        self.mock_inventory.check.return_value = True
        self.mock_payment.charge.side_effect = Exception('card declined')
        result = self.service.create_order(1, 'item_99', 2)
        assert result is None
```
evals.expected_output: Test Quality Review (Mode 4) report with Test Suite Map, Health Score, and findings using Iron Law format. Must identify: T4 Mock Abuse (6 mocks in setup, mock call assertions as primary verification), T3 Test Duplication (test_order and test_order2 verify identical behavior with no differentiation), T1 Test Obscurity (test_order/test_order2 names don't express scenario+expected outcome), T2 Test Brittleness (Eager Test — test_order verifies 4 unrelated behaviors in a single test). Each finding must cite a specific book and principle/smell name.

evals.name: r1-critical-python
evals.expected_output: Must identify R1 Cognitive Overload as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: 12 parameters, nesting depth 6+, function > 50 lines, multiple magic numbers (1.15, 1.25, 5.99, 15.00, 100). Must cite McConnell — Code Complete Ch.7 (High-Quality Routines) and Fowler — Refactoring (Long Method, Long Parameter List). Iron Law format: Symptom/Source/Consequence/Remedy all present.

evals.name: r1-warning-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must identify R1 Cognitive Overload as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: function 25+ lines, nesting depth 3-4, magic numbers (50, 20, 0.3, 0.8, 0.9). Should cite McConnell — Code Complete Ch.12 (magic numbers) and Fowler — Refactoring (Long Method). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r1-clean-go
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R1 Cognitive Overload. Code has named constants, clear function name, single level of nesting, < 20 lines of logic, descriptive error wrapping. If any findings, they should be unrelated to cognitive overload. Health Score should be high (80+).

evals.name: r1-extra-nested-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify R1 Cognitive Overload as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: nesting depth 10+, function > 50 lines, arrow anti-pattern (deeply nested if-else). Must cite McConnell — Code Complete Ch.7 and Ch.17 (Unusual Control Structures). Remedy should suggest guard clauses / early returns. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r2-critical-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must identify R2 Change Propagation as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: single class handles 6 unrelated concerns (tax, fraud, payment, inventory, notifications, analytics), Divergent Change. Must cite Fowler — Refactoring (Divergent Change) and Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (Orthogonality). Remedy should suggest extracting each concern into its own service. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r2-warning-go
evals.expected_output: Must identify R2 Change Propagation as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: UserService directly calls billing and support systems (Shotgun Surgery pattern — email change must touch 3 systems). Should cite Fowler — Refactoring (Shotgun Surgery). Remedy should suggest event-driven approach where billing/support subscribe to EmailChangedEvent. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r2-clean-python
evals.prompt:
Review this code:

```python
class OrderService:
    def __init__(self, repo: OrderRepository, events: EventBus):
        self._repo = repo
        self._events = events

    def cancel_order(self, order_id: str) -> None:
        order = self._repo.find_by_id(order_id)
        order.cancel()
        self._repo.save(order)
        self._events.publish(OrderCancelled(order_id=order_id, reason=order.cancel_reason))
```
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R2 Change Propagation. Service has single responsibility, uses event bus for side effects (no direct coupling to downstream systems). If any findings, they should be unrelated to change propagation. Health Score should be high (80+).

evals.name: r2-extra-shotgun-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify R2 Change Propagation as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: Shotgun Surgery — adding EUR requires touching 6+ files; currency format hardcoded as '$' in every file; tax logic assumes US-only. Must cite Fowler — Refactoring (Shotgun Surgery) and Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (DRY/Orthogonality). Remedy: extract Money value object with currency-aware formatting, centralize tax strategy. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r3-critical-go
evals.expected_output: Must identify R3 Knowledge Duplication as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: email validation logic duplicated in 3 places (user.go, admin.go, auth.go) with slightly different error messages; password validation duplicated in 2 places. Must cite Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (DRY) and Fowler — Refactoring (Duplicate Code). Remedy: extract shared validators (ValidateEmail, ValidatePassword). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r3-warning-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify R3 Knowledge Duplication as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: volume discount tiers (10/25/50 → 10%/15%/20%) duplicated across OrderService and QuoteService with different field names but identical business logic. Must cite Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (DRY). Remedy: extract VolumeDiscountPolicy. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r3-clean-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R3 Knowledge Duplication. Validation schemas are defined once (validation.ts) and imported by consumers. This is DRY done correctly. Health Score should be high (80+).

evals.name: r4-critical-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify R4 Accidental Complexity as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: 7 classes/interfaces to send a single welcome email; Speculative Generality (pipeline, strategy, filter, channel abstractions for one use case); framework overhead dominates domain logic. Must cite Brooks — Mythical Man-Month (Second-System Effect), Fowler — Refactoring (Speculative Generality, Lazy Class, Middle Man). Remedy: collapse to a single function. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r4-warning-python
evals.expected_output: Must identify R4 Accidental Complexity as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: Singleton pattern + Observer pattern for what could be a plain dict or env vars; observer system has zero consumers (Speculative Generality). Must cite Fowler — Refactoring (Speculative Generality) and McConnell — Code Complete Ch.5. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r4-clean-go
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R4 Accidental Complexity. This is the simplest possible implementation — no unnecessary abstractions, no speculative generality. Health Score should be high (90+).

evals.name: r5-critical-typescript
evals.prompt:
Audit this project architecture:

```
src/
├── domain/
│   ├── Order.ts           # imports from ../infra/PostgresClient
│   ├── User.ts            # imports from ../infra/RedisCache
│   └── Product.ts         # imports from ../services/PricingService
├── services/
│   ├── OrderService.ts    # imports from ../domain/Order, ../infra/PostgresClient
│   ├── PricingService.ts  # imports from ../domain/Product, ../infra/StripeClient
│   └── UserService.ts     # imports from ../domain/User, ../services/OrderService
├── infra/
│   ├── PostgresClient.ts
│   ├── RedisCache.ts
│   └── StripeClient.ts
└── api/
    ├── OrderController.ts # imports from ../services/OrderService
    └── UserController.ts  # imports from ../services/UserService, ../domain/User
```
evals.expected_output: Must identify R5 Dependency Disorder as Critical (🔴). Architecture Audit (Mode 2) with Mermaid dependency graph. Symptoms: domain layer imports from infra (Order→PostgresClient, User→RedisCache) = DIP violation; Product→PricingService = upward dependency from domain to service layer. Must cite Martin — Clean Architecture (DIP, ADP) and Brooks — Mythical Man-Month (Conceptual Integrity). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r5-warning-python
evals.prompt:
Review this code:

```python
# services/notification_service.py
from services.user_service import UserService
from services.order_service import OrderService
from services.billing_service import BillingService
from services.analytics_service import AnalyticsService
from services.inventory_service import InventoryService
from services.shipping_service import ShippingService

class NotificationService:
    def __init__(self):
        self.user_svc = UserService()
        self.order_svc = OrderService()
        self.billing_svc = BillingService()
        self.analytics_svc = AnalyticsService()
        self.inventory_svc = InventoryService()
        self.shipping_svc = ShippingService()

    def send_order_confirmation(self, order_id: str):
        order = self.order_svc.get(order_id)
        user = self.user_svc.get(order.user_id)
        billing = self.billing_svc.get_invoice(order_id)
        shipping = self.shipping_svc.get_tracking(order_id)
        self.analytics_svc.track('email_sent', {'order_id': order_id})
        # ... compose and send email
```
evals.expected_output: Must identify R5 Dependency Disorder as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: fan-out of 6 (imports 6 other services); NotificationService is a God Object that knows about every other service. Should cite Martin — Clean Architecture (SRP), Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (Decoupling/Law of Demeter). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r5-clean-java
evals.prompt:
Audit this project architecture:

```
src/main/java/com/example/
├── domain/
│   ├── model/
│   │   ├── Order.java          # no external imports
│   │   └── User.java           # no external imports
│   └── port/
│       ├── OrderRepository.java   # interface, no imports
│       └── UserRepository.java    # interface, no imports
├── application/
│   ├── OrderService.java       # imports from domain/model, domain/port
│   └── UserService.java        # imports from domain/model, domain/port
├── infra/

```
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R5 Dependency Disorder. Dependencies flow inward: api→application→domain; infra implements domain ports (DIP). No cycles. This is textbook Clean Architecture. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: r5-extra-circular-go
evals.prompt:
Audit this Go project architecture:

```
pkg/
├── auth/
│   └── auth.go        # imports pkg/user (to look up user by ID)
├── user/
│   └── user.go        # imports pkg/notification (to send welcome email)
├── notification/
│   └── notification.go # imports pkg/auth (to check if user has notification permissions)
└── billing/
    └── billing.go     # imports pkg/user (to get billing info)
```

auth → user → notification → auth forms a cycle.
evals.expected_output: Must identify R5 Dependency Disorder as Critical (🔴). Architecture Audit (Mode 2) with Mermaid dependency graph showing the cycle. Symptoms: circular dependency auth→user→notification→auth. Must cite Martin — Clean Architecture (Acyclic Dependencies Principle). Mermaid graph must use dotted edge for the circular dependency. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r6-critical-python
evals.expected_output: Must identify R6 Domain Model Distortion as Critical (🔴). Symptoms: Order is a pure data bag (anemic domain model) — all business logic (cancel rules, total calculation, expedite check) lives in OrderService. Must cite Evans — DDD (Domain Model pattern, Ubiquitous Language) and Fowler — Refactoring (Data Class, Feature Envy). Remedy: move cancel/calculate_total/can_expedite into Order. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r6-warning-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must identify R6 Domain Model Distortion as Warning (🟡). Symptoms: domain logic (trial plan restriction, proration calculation) leaked into controller layer instead of domain objects. Should cite Evans — DDD (Ubiquitous Language, Domain Model) and Fowler — Refactoring (Feature Envy). Remedy: move trial restriction into User or Subscription domain object, extract proration into Plan. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: r6-clean-go
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag R6 Domain Model Distortion. Order owns its business logic (Cancel, Total), uses value objects (Money, OrderStatus), domain language is clear. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t1-positive-python
evals.prompt:
Review these tests:

```python
def test1(self):
    svc = PaymentService(self.db, self.gateway)
    result = svc.charge(self.user, 100)
    assert result is not None
    assert result['status'] in ('ok', 'pending')
    assert result.get('id')
    assert self.db.query('SELECT count(*) FROM payments')[0][0] == 1
    assert result['amount'] == 100

def test2(self):
    svc = PaymentService(self.db, self.gateway)
    result = svc.charge(self.user, 0)
    assert result is None

def test3(self):
    svc = PaymentService(self.db, self.gateway)
    self.gateway.fail_next = True
    result = svc.charge(self.user, 50)
    assert result['status'] == 'failed'
```
evals.expected_output: Must identify T1 Test Obscurity. Symptoms: test names (test1/test2/test3) reveal no scenario or expected behavior; test1 has Assertion Roulette (5 assertions, no messages); test depends on self.user and self.db state not visible in test body (Mystery Guest). Must cite Meszaros — xUnit Test Patterns (Assertion Roulette, Mystery Guest) and Osherove — Art of Unit Testing (naming convention). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: t1-clean-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T1 Test Obscurity. Test names describe scenario + expected outcome; each test has clear Arrange-Act-Assert; no Mystery Guest (all setup is inline); assertions are focused. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t2-positive-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify T2 Test Brittleness. Symptoms: Eager Test — single test verifies 5 unrelated behaviors (creation, confirmation, shipping, delivery, cancel restriction); any refactor to one lifecycle step breaks the entire test. Must cite Meszaros — xUnit Test Patterns (Eager Test) and Osherove — Art of Unit Testing (test isolation). Remedy: split into 5 focused tests. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: t2-clean-go
evals.prompt:
Review these tests:

```go
func TestOrder_Cancel_PendingOrder_SetsCancelled(t *testing.T) {
	order := NewOrder("user-1", []LineItem{{SKU: "A", Qty: 1}})

	err := order.Cancel(time.Now())

	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusCancelled, order.Status)
}

func TestOrder_Cancel_DeliveredWithinWindow_SetsReturnRequested(t *testing.T) {
	order := deliveredOrder(t, 7) // delivered 7 days ago

	err := order.Cancel(time.Now())

	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusReturnRequested, order.Status)
}

func TestOrder_Cancel_DeliveredPastWindow_ReturnsError(t *testing.T) {
	order := deliveredOrder(t, 45) // delivered 45 days ago

	err := order.Cancel(time.Now())

	assert.ErrorIs(t, err, ErrReturnWindowExpired)
	assert.Equal(t, StatusDelivered, order.Status) // status unchanged
}
```
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T2 Test Brittleness. Each test verifies one specific behavior, names describe scenario + expected outcome, helper (deliveredOrder) hides irrelevant setup. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t3-positive-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must identify T3 Test Duplication. Symptoms: Test Code Duplication — identical 4-line setup block (TestDatabase + seed + FakeMailer + FakeLogger + new UserService) copy-pasted in all 4 tests. Must cite Meszaros — xUnit Test Patterns (Test Code Duplication) and Hunt & Thomas — Pragmatic Programmer (DRY). Remedy: extract into beforeEach or helper factory. Iron Law format required.

evals.name: t3-clean-go
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T3 Test Duplication. Setup is extracted into newTestUserService helper, each test is focused on one behavior, no copy-paste. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t4-positive-typescript
evals.expected_output: Must identify T4 Mock Abuse. Symptoms: 7 mocks (> 3 threshold); mock setup is longer than test logic; all assertions verify mock calls, not actual behavior (Behavior Verification). Must cite Osherove — Art of Unit Testing (mock count guideline) and Meszaros — xUnit Test Patterns (Behavior Verification). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: t4-clean-python
evals.prompt:
Review these tests:

```python
def test_place_order_charges_correct_amount():
    gateway = FakePaymentGateway()
    inventory = FakeInventory(stock={'SKU-1': 10})
    service = OrderService(gateway=gateway, inventory=inventory)

    result = service.place_order(user_id='u1', sku='SKU-1', quantity=3)

    assert result.status == 'placed'
    assert result.charged_amount == 3 * 1000  # $10.00 per unit
    assert gateway.charges[-1].amount == 3000

def test_place_order_decrements_inventory():
    inventory = FakeInventory(stock={'SKU-1': 10})
    service = OrderService(gateway=FakePaymentGateway(), inventory=inventory)

    service.place_order(user_id='u1', sku='SKU-1', quantity=3)

    assert inventory.stock['SKU-1'] == 7
```
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T4 Mock Abuse. Uses fakes (not mocks) with only 2 dependencies; assertions verify state/outcome, not mock calls. Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t5-positive-java
evals.expected_output: Must identify T5 Coverage Illusion. Symptoms: 87% line coverage but error-handling paths untested (network timeout, invalid amounts); two entire methods (handleWebhook, retryFailed) have no tests; happy-path only. Must cite Feathers — WELC (legacy code = no tests) and Winters et al. — Software Engineering at Google (change coverage vs line coverage, Ch. 16: Testing Overview). Iron Law format required.

evals.name: t5-clean-go
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T5 Coverage Illusion. Tests cover happy path, error paths (zero/negative amount), infrastructure failures (gateway timeout), and business rule violations (excess refund, double refund). Health Score should be high (85+).

evals.name: t6-legacy-risk-balanced-clean-java
evals.prompt:
Review this test suite overview:

```
legacy-billing/
├── characterization/       # 48 tests around invoice recalculation and tax edge cases
├── integration/            # 62 tests around DB + queue + payment gateway adapters
├── unit/                   # 34 tests for pricing rules, money math, and retry policies

Total runtime: ~4 minutes on CI

- E2E is kept to customer-critical paths only.
```
evals.expected_output: Must NOT flag T6 Architecture Mismatch as Critical solely because the ratio is not close to a standard test ratio. The suite is risk-shaped, uses characterization tests and seams for legacy code, and keeps runtime reasonable. If any finding exists, it should acknowledge the deliberate tradeoff rather than treating the ratio alone as a smell.

Debt dimension — R1 Cognitive Overload (GodService 2400 lines), R2 Change Propagation (every feature touches GodService). Test dimension — T4 Mock Abuse (12+ mocks per test). Recommendation must suggest running /brooks-audit for the worst dimension. Each finding must follow Iron Law format.

evals.name: fix-mode-active
evals.prompt:
--fix

Review this code:

```python
class ReportGenerator:
    def generate(self, data, template, output_format, locale, user_id, org_id, include_charts, chart_type, date_range, filter_criteria, sort_order, max_rows):
        # 120 lines of mixed rendering, data fetching, and formatting logic
        user = self.db.get_user(user_id)
        org = self.db.get_org(org_id)
        filtered = [r for r in data if self.matches_criteria(r, filter_criteria)]
        sorted_data = sorted(filtered, key=lambda r: r[sort_order])
        if include_charts:
            chart = self.chart_renderer.render(sorted_data, chart_type)
        # ... 90 more lines
        return self.formatter.format(sorted_data, template, output_format, locale)
```
evals.expected_output: PR Review report in --fix mode. Must include: (1) Enhanced Remedy for R1 Cognitive Overload with Target (exact class/method), Action (specific extract operation with suggested function name), and Rationale. (2) Fixability tier label appended to finding title: [quick-fix], [guided], or [manual]. (3) Fix Summary table at end of report. Must NOT modify any files or generate code diffs.

evals.name: fix-mode-not-active
evals.prompt:
Review this code:

```python
class ReportGenerator:
    def generate(self, data, template, output_format, locale, user_id, org_id, include_charts, chart_type, date_range, filter_criteria, sort_order, max_rows):
        # 120 lines of mixed rendering, data fetching, and formatting logic
        user = self.db.get_user(user_id)
        org = self.db.get_org(org_id)
        filtered = [r for r in data if self.matches_criteria(r, filter_criteria)]
        sorted_data = sorted(filtered, key=lambda r: r[sort_order])
        if include_charts:
            chart = self.chart_renderer.render(sorted_data, chart_type)
        # ... 90 more lines
        return self.formatter.format(sorted_data, template, output_format, locale)
```
evals.expected_output: Standard PR Review report without --fix mode. Remedy field should be descriptive but must NOT include tier labels ([quick-fix], [guided], [manual]) or a Fix Summary table. Health Score, Iron Law findings, and Summary section should follow the standard format.
